_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use comprehension strategies to understand texts we listen to, watch and read — and to discuss our understanding with others.

_source.w: We are learning to use comprehension strategies to understand texts we listen to, watch and read — and to discuss our understanding with others.
y: Comprehension strategies help us get the most out of every text we encounter.
Discussing our understanding with others deepens our thinking and reveals new perspectives.
These strategies make us independent, thoughtful readers.
eg: We visualise scenes as we read and describe what we see in our minds.
We make predictions before and during reading and check them afterwards.
We ask questions about confusing parts and work to answer them.
We summarise the main idea of a text in our own words.
We make connections between the text and our own experiences, other texts or the world.
We discuss our understanding with a partner and compare our interpretations.
